channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Trees overhanging on to pavement, 13th May
item.description:
On corner of abbotswood road/ ermin street behind doctors surgery
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Abbotswood Road, Brockworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Tree causing damage/obstruction to pavement or road

item.title: INADEQUATE CLEARANCE & FAILED INSPECTION: Critical Skid Hazard Remains, 13th May
item.description:
I am updating this report following a partial attempt by the council to clear the lane yesterday. While I appreciate that approximately 200kg of grit was removed, this has barely addressed the scale of the problem. The crew on-site explicitly stated that significantly more work is required but couldn’t stipulate when or for sure. **1. Continuing Safety Risk:** Despite the partial sweep, a substantial layer of fine silt and loose grit remains across the steep gradient and blind junction. This continues to act as a "ball-bearing" skid hazard for vehicles and cyclists. A 4-year-old child has already been injured here; leaving the job half-finished is a failure of the Highway Authority’s duty of care. **2. Inadequate Safety Inspection:** A Highways Safety Inspector attended the site today. However, they were observed performing a three-point turn and leaving without a thorough foot inspection. Critically, at the time of their visit, a vehicle was parked over the largest accumulation of debris, meaning the inspector could not have accurately assessed the depth of the silt or the severity of the skid risk. **3. Health and Property Impact:** As the weather remains dry, the remaining silt is being whipped up into clouds of fine dust. We are still unable to open our windows due to the respiratory health risk and the threat of damage to sensitive computing equipment within our home. **Required Action:** This cannot be closed as "resolved." I require a full mechanical sweep to be completed immediately to remove the remaining hundreds of kilograms of debris. I also request a re-inspection on foot when the road surface is clear of parked vehicles to ensure the "intervention criteria" for a hazardous road surface are correctly met. I am keeping a log of this correspondence for escalation to the Local Government Ombudsman should this jurisdictional buck-passing continue to compromise public safety.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Green Street, Brockworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped items, 13th May
item.description:
Black bag, cone, sign dumped in the corner of the car park area for the houses on Stearman Walk, access to the car park is via Roselle Drive then Linden Close Brockworth.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Linden Close, Hucclecote, Tewkesbury

The tree concerned is situated inbetween the two giant redwoods. It looks dead.

item.title: Deep and wide pothole outside our drive, 13th May
item.description:
127 Paygrove Lane on the road directly outside next to the curb.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Paygrove Lane, Longlevens, Gloucester
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: All Saints Road, Cheltenham is full of 100's of potholes and sinkholes, 13th May
item.description:
The whole length of All Saints Road, Cheltenham is full of potholes and sinkholes. Some of these have been patched terribly, are not evenly repaired or are sinkholes which are continuing to sink, and present a clear danger to cyclists or motorcyclists. For motorists, there is a problem with going down badly patched sinkholes and other badly patched repairs. There are about 100 so its impossible to send you photos - you need to come and survey the whole length of the road.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 41, All Saints' Road, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flickering street light., 13th May
item.description:
Outside 24 Hartlebury Way, Charlton Kings.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 28, Hartlebury Way, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree hitting overhead telephone/broadband cable, 13th May
item.description:
The tree growing in the adjacent verge is growing into my overhead telephone/broadband cable at 18 Cakebridge Road risking cable breakage, especially during high winds. This has been becoming progressively worse as the tree grows and looks like this year’s growth may overwhelm the cable. Please take urgent corrective action to cut the tree back before the cable is broken. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 18, Cakebridge Road, Lynworth, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Light sensor is not detecting vehicles problem, 13th May
item.description:
Works ref: HM7532534959-02 Seems like light system is not working. Out of sync and may have been twisted around. Someone needs to take a look at these lights.
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Light sensor is not detecting vehicles

item.title: Curb has come away a from the path, 13th May
item.description:
This is directly outside 15 Orchard Way. The high rise curb in front of the parking space has eroded away. It would be appreciated to consider upon fixing the issue to lower the curb to prevent this issue reoccurring. Thank you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Orchard Way, Churchdown, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown tree causing blind spots, 13th May
item.description:
These trees are over gown around the bottom and causing blind spots when pulling out of sydenham road north junction. If a cyclist/motor bike was in one of these blind spots they would not be seen by a car at the junction
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Cranham Road, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Vegetation obstructing view when pulling out of junction, 13th May
item.description:
The trees on both sides of this junction are very overgrown and leafy at the bottom. When pulling out onto the main road there is very little/no visibility of oncoming traffic, the growth on the trees is causing big blind spots
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Cranham Road, Ewens Farm, Cheltenham, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
